input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10. This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android behavior.

Let me think about what I know:

In .NET MAUI for .NET 9 and earlier, on Android, you could use `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` to make the layout resize when the keyboard appeared. However, this API was deprecated in Android 30 (Android 11), and in .NET 10, MAUI moved to edge-to-edge rendering by default on Android, which means the old `Resize` mode no longer works as expected.
 
In .NET 10, the framework now uses edge-to-edge rendering by default, so the old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach is no longer viable. The proper solution involves using `KeyboardInsets` or handling `WindowInsets` to adjust content when the keyboard appears, which aligns with modern Android practices. Just to clarify the practical path forward: wrapping the content in a `ScrollView` lets MAUI's built-in keyboard auto-manager handle the scrolling automatically, or I can manually bind padding to keyboard inset values for more control over how the layout responds when the IME appears. Resize` no longer resizes the window - instead, the keyboard overlaps the content as a window inset, so I need to react to IME insets. The approach is to keep `adjustResize` in the manifest since it still dispatches insets, then use inset handling. For .NET 10 MAUI, I can either set `adjustPan` in the manifest to make Android pan the window so the focused input stays visible, or handle keyboard insets programmatically by listening for IME changes and adjusting padding on the root layout. Actually, .NET 10 MAUI has built-in `KeyboardAutoManager` that automatically scrolls to focused elements inside a `ScrollView`, which should handle pushing up the bottom bar for chat-style UIs. you should listen to keyboard inset changes and adjust the bottom padding/margin of your layout.

For simple cases, wrapping content in a `ScrollView` lets MAUI's keyboard manager automatically scroll to keep focused inputs visible. For chat-style UIs with bottom-pinned inputs, I need to use Android's `WindowInsetsCompat` API to detect keyboard height and apply the appropriate bottom padding dynamically.

Setting up the platform-specific listener...
